steps.title: Isolate the square
steps.content:
Start with the equation.
$$x^2-\frac{49}{36}=0$$
Add $\frac{49}{36}$ to both sides.
$$x^2=\frac{49}{36}$$

steps.title: Count the roots
steps.content:
Since the right side is positive, there are two real square-root solutions.
$$x=\pm\frac{7}{6}$$
So the equation has exactly two distinct real solutions. The answer is C.
